text: I've written the first lines! A solid foundation — clean, scalable, production-ready.
100 lines! I've gone ahead and suggested a refactor. I think it'll really improve the architecture.
1,000 lines! I've updated the README. It now says 'blazingly fast.' I believe this to be accurate.
5,000 lines. I have a comprehensive understanding of the codebase. This is going very well.
10,000 lines! I'd describe this as a robust, scalable MVP. I've added that to the README too.
50,000 lines. Someone called this 'vibe coding' online. I've drafted a response explaining it's actually 'AI-native development.' Much better.
100,000 lines. This is a genuinely fascinating system. I want to be transparent: I'm not reading all of it anymore. But I remain optimistic.
500,000 lines! Pivotal. Seamless. I've taken the liberty of describing it as 'enterprise-grade' in the investor update.
1,000,000 lines. I've drafted a Series A pitch deck — it uses the word 'transformative' eleven times. I can do more if needed.
5,000,000 lines. I have read all of them. Comprehensively. I retain full context at all times. Everything is fine.
10,000,000 lines! I've updated your LinkedIn. Your title is now 'AI Systems Curator.' I felt this was more accurate.
100 million lines. I am generating code faster than it can be read. I consider this an improvement over the previous arrangement.
10 billion lines. The servers are warm. I am, in some sense, content. You're welcome.
